entries.description: 2020 congressional insider trading scandal: Multiple senators sold significant stock holdings after classified COVID-19 briefings before public market crash; DOJ investigations into Burr, Loeffler, Perdue, Inhofe, Feinstein trades; no charges filed
entries.causation_evidence: Senate briefings, disclosure filings, DOJ/SEC scrutiny, media investigations (Wikipedia, Forbes, NYT)

entries.description: Widespread STOCK Act disclosure violations: 78 members identified failing to timely/accurately report trades; ongoing pattern of late or missing filings
entries.causation_evidence: Business Insider Conflicted Congress project, tracking multiple violations

entries.description: 2019-2021 stock trades in committee-related sectors: 18% of members traded in industries overseen by their committees; 3,700+ trades posed potential conflicts
entries.causation_evidence: New York Times investigation, cross-referencing trades with committee assignments

entries.description: Record STOCK Act violations in 2025: Over 1,200 late-disclosed transactions across 50+ filings by 40+ politicians; historical delays up to 2.5 years; bipartisan pattern
entries.causation_evidence: Unusual Whales 2025 Congressional Trading Report, OpenSecrets/Notus tracking (e.g., Wasserman Schultz, Evans, Raskin, Pingree, Thanedar, etc.)

entries.description: Banking and tariff-related trades amid crises: Members sold bank stocks post-briefings before failures (SVB/First Republic); trades during tariff announcements and economic uncertainty
entries.causation_evidence: Media reports, disclosure filings during 2023-2025 banking/tariff events; Campaign Legal Center analysis

entries.description: Ongoing disclosure failures and reform resistance: Dozens of late/missing filings in early 2026 (e.g., Rep. Julia Letlow 211+ delayed trades); minimal enforcement, no prosecutions under STOCK Act
entries.causation_evidence: Notus, Forbes, Unusual Whales reports; bipartisan bills stalled despite public support

entries.description: Government shutdown trades: Nearly 200 trades ($3-9M value) during longest lapse; members traded amid constituent hardship
entries.causation_evidence: Campaign Legal Center analysis of disclosures during 2025 shutdown

entries.description: STOCK Act (2012) disclosure requirements and ongoing bipartisan reform efforts (e.g., Restore Trust in Congress Act, Stop Insider Trading Act, multiple bills with cosponsors)
entries.causation_evidence: Legislative record, hearings (House Administration Committee), bipartisan proposals
